rationale: In the related-literature discussion, the cited paper is not only listed but linked to a specific prior result: ad features affect product sales through visual attention (gaze duration). The citing paper uses that result to position its own contribution in the same attention-effectiveness stream and to motivate linking attention to downstream outcomes. While brief, this is a meaningful framing role rather than a purely generic mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends prior attention-to-sales work represented by Zhang, Wedel, and Pieters (2009) by moving to an online news context and studying spillovers from article attention to ad attention. It then connects this incremental ad attention to recall and incentivized purchase outcomes in its experimental design.
